MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Aug-18-2023 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Timothy C. Pieper of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6170), appearing for defendant Genuine Parts Company.
Hanna M. Thompson of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Riley Power Inc.
Noorvik Minasin of DLA PIPER (US), LLP (310-595-3000), appearing for defendant 3M Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is May 8, 2023 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is May 19, 2023. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 4, 2023. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 21, 2023. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 5, 2023.All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 16, 2023. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than February 2, 2023, plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than February 2, 2023, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 16, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 24, 2023. Responses to the demand are due no later than April 7, 2023. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by April 28, 2023 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, May 4, 2023 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)
